check_attribution_support.note: Attribution is to ru/gru/74455/sandworm at 'high' (ESET/Mandiant/CERT-UA), but the cited ESET source explicitly states 'ESET Research has not yet been able to attribute these attacks to a known threat actor' and never names Sandworm. The Sandworm attribution came later/from Mandiant+CERT-UA, which are not the fetched source. As fetched, the ESET source does NOT support a high-confidence Sandworm attribution.

check_targets_match.note: Ukrainian government targeting is confirmed ('a Ukrainian governmental network'); orgs/ukraine-finance-sector is NOT confirmed in the fetched text ('finance' absent). Finance target is an extrapolation here.

check_doctrine_honest.note: ru/russkiy-mir marked attested with reasoning citing 'Ukrainian government and finance' — government is supported but finance is not in the source, the source does not attribute to Russia/Sandworm, and it names no doctrine. Attested overreaches; downgrade to strongly_inferred. (ru/military-doctrine-2014 strongly_inferred is fine.)

second_source_needed: For the Sandworm attribution, add the Mandiant or CERT-UA attribution source (the ESET source explicitly declines to attribute); a finance-sector targeting source is also needed
draft_qc_note: ESET source strongly supports the technical event but explicitly does NOT attribute to Sandworm and does not mention finance-sector targeting. Add the Mandiant/CERT-UA attribution source, drop/soften the finance target, and downgrade the attested Russkiy-Mir link.
